step1.title: Application information
step1.proposedFuelCodeEffectiveHelp: (optional, if left blank, default is the date of application receipt by the Ministry)
step1.deleteConfirmText: This will permanently remove the draft. This action cannot be undone.

validation.capacityPositive: Facility nameplate capacity must be greater than zero.

step2.title: Proposed fuel pathways
step2.descriptionLabel: Description of distinctive or unique characteristics of the fuel pathway(s) included in this application
step2.descriptionHelp: e.g. use of carbon capture and sequestration (CCS), novel energy sources for fuel production, upgrades made to facility (for fuel code renewal applications), etc.

validation.atLeastOneRow: At least one pathway is required.
validation.fixSpecificFields: {{count}} pathway row(s) have missing or invalid values. Please complete: {{fields}}.
validation.dateOrder: Operating data collection end date must be on or after the start date. Please fix the dates and try again.

step3.title: Documents & GHGenius modelling

bullets.notification: Notification of representation agreement form (optional, required if applicable)

step3.otherPlaceholder: Brief description of other supporting documentation included
step3.ghgeniusIntro: Download the Excel template below and complete it with the required modelling input and output tables. When completed, upload the Excel spreadsheet.

modellingInstructions.header: Provide model inputs/outputs for each fuel pathway in the tables provided in the Excel template

inputs.region: Ensure model region is stated for each model run
inputs.grouping: Group inputs by the sheet they are located in (e.g. group together all inputs of the fuel production run entered in the Coprods sheet)
inputs.ordering: Ensure that the inputs are ordered sequentially by their location in the respective sheet (e.g. cell AZ241 should be above cell AZ243)
inputs.descriptors: Include GHGenius column and row descriptors in the description (e.g. instead of "feedstock transport" state "corn oil rail distance" which identifies the column "corn oil" and row "rail")

outputs.copyFromTab: Where possible, copy results directly from the BC LCFS Tab in GHGenius
outputs.columnLabel: Provide column label in the Results Location field for results pulled from GHGenius
outputs.outOfModel: For results from out of model calculations state 'out of model calc.' in the Results Location field and ensure the calculations are included in the Technical Report
outputs.sampleTable: A sample output table is provided in the Appendix of

errors.unsupportedType: Unsupported file type. Allowed: PDF, Word, Excel, CSV, plain text, PNG, JPEG.
errors.missingRequired: Technical report and GHGenius model uploads are required.

step4.title: Sign & submit
step4.consultantConsentLabel: (optional) I consent to the Ministry communicating directly with a consultant/third-party to address questions or requests for additional information related to the Application or Technical report. Fill out form below.

declarations.informationTrue: I certify that the information submitted is true and complete and the records regarding this application have been retained in accordance with the Low Carbon Fuels Act.
declarations.response8Weeks: I acknowledge that requests for additional information and/or corrections must be responded to within 8 weeks. Failure to respond within this period may result in rejection of the application, requiring the applicant to restart the application process and resulting in a deferred Effective Date.
declarations.section206: Pursuant to section 20(6) of the Low Carbon Fuels Act, I acknowledge my obligation to provide written notice to the Director if I become aware that the details of the fuel pathway(s) included in this application have changed or will change.

validation.allDeclarationsRequired: All three declarations must be acknowledged before submitting.
validation.consultantFieldsRequired: Consultant name, company, and email are required when consenting to consultant communication.
validation.consultantEmailInvalid: Enter a valid consultant email address.

step4.submitConfirmText: Once submitted you will not be able to edit Steps 1-4. Continue?

step5.title: Government decision
step5.intro: Ministry staff will evaluate the application to ensure the methodology and assumptions made are acceptable, and that the carbon intensity can be reproduced. If an error or omission is identified in the application, the applicant will be asked to submit additional information.
step5.responseExpectations: Applicants must respond to requests for additional information and/or corrections within 8 weeks. Failure to respond within this period may result in rejection of the application.
step5.addCommentLabel: Comments to government staff to support your application (optional):
step5.uploadDisabledHelp: If government requires additional documentation the button below will be enabled to add or replace documents to your application.
step5.commentTooLong: Comment must be 4000 characters or fewer.

stepStub.comingSoon: This step is not yet available.
stepStub.description: This part of the workflow will be implemented in a future task. The accordion above keeps your place in the wizard.

footer:
For security and privacy, please don't share personal information on this website.
This information does not replace or constitute legal advice. Users are responsible for ensuring compliance with the Low Carbon Fuels Act and Regulations.
